channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Putin apologizes for 'tragic incident' but stops short of saying Azerbaijani plane was shot down
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russian President Vladimir Putin on Saturday apologized to his Azerbaijani counterpart for what he called a “tragic incident” following the crash of an Azerbaijani airliner in Kazakhstan that killed 38 people, but stopped short of acknowledging that Moscow was responsible.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Musk writes op-ed backing far-right German political party
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk penned an op-ed further expressing support for the far-right German political party Alternative for Germany (AfD). In response, the paper’s commentary editor resigned in protest.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Drought, fires and deforestation battered Amazon rainforest in 2024
item.description: BOGOTA, Colombia (AP) — 2024 was a brutal year for the Amazon rainforest, with rampant wildfires and extreme drought ravaging large parts of a biome that’s a critical counterweight to climate change. A warming climate fed drought that in turn fed the worst year for fires since 2005.
item.dc:creator: STEVEN GRATTAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Kazakhstan plane crash survivors say they heard bangs before aircraft went down, Putin issues statement
item.description: Survivors of the Azerbaijan Airlines plane that crashed in Kazakhstan on Christmas Day say they heard at least one loud bang before the aircraft crashed.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former Indian Prime Minister Manmohan Singh, who instituted economic reforms, cremated in New Delhi
item.description: Manmohan Singh, the former Indian prime minister widely regarded as the architect of the country’s economic reform program, has been cremated after a state funeral.
item.dc:creator: Aijaz Hussain, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Leon Panetta suggests it's 'pretty clear' Russia 'responsible' for Azerbaijan Airlines plane crash
item.description: Former Secretary of Defense and CIA director Leon Panetta speculated that Russian air defenses may have caused the crash of an Azerbaijan Airlines plane in Kazakhstan that killed 38 people and left all 29 survivors injured earlier this week.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: Man on vacation with family goes overboard on Norwegian cruise ship in Bahamas
item.description: Authorities have called off the search for a 51-year-old man who went overboard Thursday on a Norwegian Cruise Line ship in the Western Caribean.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NATO will step up Baltic Sea patrols as Finland probes the possible sabotage of undersea cables
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte said Friday that the military alliance will step up patrols in the Baltic Sea region as Finnish investigators work to establish whether a ship linked to Russia sabotaged undersea cables there this week.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: SEE IT: China stuns with maiden flight of sixth-generation aircraft
item.description: China appeared to unveil it's newest sixth-generation fighter aircraft after social media video and photos showed the tailless aircraft soaring above.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korea's opposition-controlled National Assembly votes to impeach acting President Han
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s opposition-controlled National Assembly voted Friday to impeach acting President Han Duck-soo despite vehement protests by governing party lawmakers, further deepening the country’s political crisis set off by President Yoon Suk Yeol’s stunning imposition of m
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mexico tests app for migrants to send alert if they're about to be detained in US
item.description: U.S. authorities are obliged to give notice to home-country consulates when a foreign citizen is detained. Mexico says it has beefed up consular staff and legal aid to help migrants in the legal process related to deportation.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US teacher jailed in Russia is formally designated as wrongfully detained
item.description: The State Department says an American schoolteacher arrested in Russia on drug charges more than three years ago has been designated by the U.S. government as wrongfully detained.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: China unveils world's largest amphibious warship
item.description: China has launched a new amphibious assault ship, named the Sichuan, which is capable of launching fighter jets and is designed to compete with U.S. military strength in the seas.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Croatia's outspoken president is seeking reelection. He faces several contenders in Sunday's vote
item.description: ZAGREB, Croatia (AP) — Croatia’s left-leaning president, an outspoken critic of Western military support for Ukraine in its war against Russia, is running for reelection this weekend against an array of contenders, including the candidate backed by the conservative government.
item.dc:creator: SAŠA KAVIĆ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: India's former prime minister Manmohan Singh, architect of economic reforms, dies at 92
item.description: NEW DELHI (AP) — India’s former Prime Minister Manmohan Singh, widely regarded as the architect of India’s economic reform program and a landmark nuclear deal with the United States, has died. He was 92.
item.dc:creator: AIJAZ HUSSAIN and ASHOK SHARMA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli attorney general orders probe into report that alleged Netanyahu's wife harassed opponents
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel’s attorney general has ordered police to open an investigation into Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s wife on suspicion of harassing political opponents and a witness in the Israeli leader’s corruption trial.
item.dc:creator: JOSEF FEDERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Investigation is underway in Kazakhstan plane crash
item.description: U.S. officials say Russia could be behind a passenger plane crash in Kazakhstan that left dozens dead Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Maddie Biertempfel, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Musk, Ramaswamy defend Silicon Valley's foreign-born hires
item.description: Elon Musk and Vivek Ramaswamy, co-chairs of President-elect Trump’s new “Department of Government Efficiency,” are defending the tech industry’s reliance on foreign-born engineers as the incoming Trump administration prepares to crack down on immigration.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: NATO calls for investigation into Azerbaijan Airlines crash
item.description: NATO called for a formal investigation Thursday into the Azerbaijan Airlines plane crash that killed 38 people in Kazakhstan. “Our thoughts and prayers are with the families and victims of #AzerbaijanAirlines flight #J28243.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Russia suspected in Azerbaijan Airlines crash; black boxes recovered
item.description: The U.S. officials added that if the preliminary information proves to be true, it would further underscore Russian recklessness in its invasion of Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Russia cautions against 'hypotheses' in Azerbaijan Airlines crash
item.description: Moscow on Thursday warned against speculation that Russian air defenses may have caused the crash of an Azerbaijan Airlines plane in Kazakhstan this week that killed 38 people.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: US citizen imprisoned in Russia given new 15-year sentence in wake of espionage conviction
item.description: A Russian-born U.S. citizen who was already behind bars in Russia on a bribery conviction has been handed a new 15-year sentence for espionage.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fire alarm scare forces evacuations from Eiffel Tower
item.description: The entire tower was closed temporarily, but access to the first two floors was quickly restored, according to Newsweek.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, NewsNation

item.title: Netanyahu warns Houthis amid calls for Israel to wipe out terror leadership as it did with Nasrallah, Sinwar
item.description: Former Israeli officials discuss the possibility of Jerusalem targeting the Houthi leadership in the same way it took out other terror leaders from Hamas and Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 2 Navy pilots shot down over Red Sea in apparent 'friendly fire' incident: US military
item.description: Two U.S. Navy pilots were shot down Sunday over the Red Sea in an apparent “friendly fire” incident, the U.S military said, marking the most serious incident to threaten troops in over a year of America targeting Yemen's Houthi rebels.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: China says US is 'playing with fire' after latest military aid for Taiwan
item.description: U.S. military sales and assistance aim to help Taiwan defend itself and deter China from launching an attack.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation
